questions.q: The minimum value of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>α</mi></math> for which the equation <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfrac><mn>4</mn><mrow><mi>sin</mi><mi>x</mi></mrow></mfrac><mo>+</mo><mfrac><mn>1</mn><mrow><mn>1</mn><mo>-</mo><mi>sin</mi><mi>x</mi></mrow></mfrac><mo>=</mo><mi>α</mi></math> has at least one solution in <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfenced><mrow><mn>0</mn><mo>,</mo><mfrac><mi>π</mi><mn>2</mn></mfrac></mrow></mfenced></math> is______.
questions.solution:
Let

fx=4sinx+11-sinx

⇒fx=4sinx+1+sinx1-sin2x

⇒fx=4cosecx+sec2x+tanxsecx

⇒f'x=-4cosxsin2x+2sinx+1cos3x+sin2xcos3x

⇒f'x=-4cosxsin2x+sinx+12cos3x

⇒f''x=-4-sin3x-2sinxcos2xsin4x+2sinx+1cos4x+3cos2xsinxsinx+12cos6x

⇒f''x=-4-sin3x-2sinx1-sin2xsin4x+2sinx+11-sin2x2+31-sin2xsinxsinx+121-sin2x3

For critical points,

f'x=0

⇒sinx+12cos3x=4cosxsin2x

⇒sin2xsinx+12=4cos4x

⇒sinxsinx+1=2cos2x

⇒sin2x+sinx=2-2sin2x

⇒3sin2x+sinx-2=0

⇒3sin2x+3sinx-2sinx-2=0

⇒3sinx-2sinx+1=0

⇒sinx=23

Since, sinx>0 ∀x∈0,π2

Now, at sinx=23, we get

⇒f''x=-4-827-431-491681+2×531-492+31-49×23×2591-493

⇒f''x=42827×8116+2×53592+359×23×259593>0

Hence, it is the point of minima.

∴ fxmin=423+11-23=9

fxmax→∞

fx is continuous function

∴ αmin=9

questions.q: The number of distinct real roots of the equation <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mi>x</mi><mn>5</mn></msup><mfenced><mrow><msup><mi>x</mi><mn>3</mn></msup><mo>-</mo><msup><mi>x</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>-</mo><mi>x</mi><mo>+</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></mfenced><mo>+</mo><mi>x</mi><mfenced><mrow><mn>3</mn><msup><mi>x</mi><mn>3</mn></msup><mo>-</mo><mn>4</mn><msup><mi>x</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>-</mo><mn>2</mn><mi>x</mi><mo>+</mo><mn>4</mn></mrow></mfenced><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn><mo>=</mo><mn>0</mn></math> is
questions.solution:
Given,

x5x3-x2-x+1+x3x3-4x2-2x+4-1=0

Now on rearranging we get

⇒x8-x7-x6+x5+3x4-4x3-2x2+4x-1=0

⇒x7x-1-x5x-1+3x3x-1-xx2-1-2xx-1+x-1=0

⇒x-1x7-x5+3x3-xx+1-2x+1=0

⇒x-1x5x2-1+3xx2-1-1x2-1=0

⇒x2-1x-1x5+3x-1=0

From above equation it is clearly visible that the root are x=±1 and for x5+3x-1

Let fx=x5+3x-1, f'x=5x4+3

f'x>0∀x∈R, hence it is monotonic in nature so it will have only one root other than 1 & -1.

Hence 3 real distinct roots.

If <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>m</mi></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>M</mi></math> are respectively the number of points of local minimum and local maximum of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>f</mi></math> in the interval <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfenced><mrow><mn>0</mn><mo>,</mo><mn>4</mn></mrow></mfenced></math>, then <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>m</mi><mo>+</mo><mi>M</mi></math> is equal to _____.
questions.solution:
Given,

fx=x-1x+1x-3+x-3

fx=x-3x2,3≤x≤4x-32-x2,1≤x<3x-3x2,0<x<1

f'x=3x2-6x,3<x<4-3x2+6x+2,1<x<33x2-6x,0<x<1

f'3+>0f'3-<0→ Minimum

f'1+>0f'1-<0→ Minimum

For x∈1,3,f'x=0 at one point → Maximum

For x∈3,4, f'x≠0

For x∈0,1, f'x+0

So points of minima are 2, so m=2

and point of maxima is 1, so M=1

So, M+n=3

questions.solution:
Given e2x-11ex-45ex+812=0

e3x-11e2x+812ex-45=0

Let ex=t, so t3-11t2+812t-45=0

Product of roots t1t2t3=45

i.e. ex1×ex2×ex3=45 

ex1+x2+x3=45

Given sum of root =lnP

So P=45

questions.q: Let the function <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>f</mi><mfenced><mi>x</mi></mfenced><mo>=</mo><mn>2</mn><msup><mi>x</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>-</mo><msub><mi>log</mi><mi>e</mi></msub><mi>x</mi><mo>,</mo><mi>x</mi><mo>></mo><mn>0</mn></math>, be decreasing in <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfenced><mrow><mn>0</mn><mo>,</mo><mi>a</mi></mrow></mfenced></math> and increasing in <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfenced><mrow><mi>a</mi><mo>,</mo><mn>4</mn></mrow></mfenced></math>. A tangent to the parabola <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mi>y</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>=</mo><mn>4</mn><mi>a</mi><mi>x</mi></math> at a point <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>P</mi></math> on it passes through the point <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfenced><mrow><mn>8</mn><mi>a</mi><mo>,</mo><mo> </mo><mn>8</mn><mi>a</mi><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></mfenced></math> but does not pass through the point <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfenced><mrow><mo>-</mo><mfrac><mn>1</mn><mi>a</mi></mfrac><mo>,</mo><mn>0</mn></mrow></mfenced></math>. If the equation of the normal at <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>P</mi></math> is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfrac><mi>x</mi><mi>α</mi></mfrac><mo>+</mo><mfrac><mi>y</mi><mi>β</mi></mfrac><mo>=</mo><mn>1</mn></math>, then <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>α</mi><mo>+</mo><mi>β</mi></math> is equal to
questions.solution:
Given, fx=2x2-logex

⇒f'x=4x-1x

⇒f'x=4x2-1x

⇒f'x=0⇒4x2-1=0⇒x=±12

But given x>0 so x=12

So function is decreasing in 0,12 and increasing in the interval 12,∞

So, a=12

Now equation of parabola will be y2=2x

Now tangent to y2=2x will be given by,

y=mx+12m, given this tangent passes through 8a,8a-1≡4,3,

So 3=4m+12m

⇒m=12 or 14

So equation of tangent are y=x2+1 or y=x4+2

But y=x2+1 passes through -2,0 so rejected as given in the question.

Now equation of normal at P will be,

y=-4x-212-4-12-43 as slope of normal =-114=-4

⇒y=-4x+4+32

⇒y+4x=36

⇒x9+y36=1

So, α=9,β=36

So, α+β=45

⇒A=42-12-92-18+0+83+2×1-03

⇒6A=41.

questions.q:
Let f:R→R be a function defined as fx=31-|x|2if|x|≤20if|x|>2.

Let g:R→R be given by g(x)=f(x+2)-f(x-2). If n and m denote the number of points in R where g is not continuous and not differentiable, respectively, then n+m is equal to ________.
questions.solution:
The given function is fx=31-|x|2if|x|≤20if|x|>2

By using the definition of modulus function, we have x=   x,x≥0-x, x<0 and also, we know that if x≤a, ⇒ -a≤x≤a and if x>a, ⇒ x<-a or x>a,

⇒fx=31+x2,-2≤x≤031-x2,0<x≤20,-2>x or x>2

Now, fx-2=31+x-22,-2≤x-2≤031-x-22,0<x-2≤20,-2>x-2 or x-2>2

⇒fx-2=3x2,0≤x≤26-3x2,2<x≤40,0>x or x>4

And, fx+2=31+x+22,-2≤x+2≤031-x+22,0<x+2≤20,-2>x+2 or x+2>2

⇒fx+2=6+3x2,-4≤x≤-2-3x2,-2<x≤00,-4>x or x>0

Hence, gx=fx+2-fx-2=3x2+6,             -4≤x≤-2-3x2,             -2<x<23x2-6,            2≤x≤40,            x∈(-∞,-4)∪(4,∞)

The graph of gx is given below

From the graph we can observe that the function gx is continuous everywhere, hence the number of points where gx is not continuos is n=0 and the graph has sharp corners at the points -4, 0, 4, 0, -2, 3 & 2, -3 thus the number of points where gx is not differentiable are m=4

⇒n+m=4.

questions.solution:

 We get, lny38+y2=2ln2⇒y38+y2=4

If <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>a</mi></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>b</mi></math> are respectively the <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>x</mi><mo>-</mo></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>y</mi><mo>-</mo></math>intercept of the tangent to the curve at <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>x</mi><mo>=</mo><mn>0</mn></math>, then the value of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>a</mi><mo>-</mo><mn>4</mn><mi>b</mi></math> is equal to _______.
questions.solution:
Given,

dydx-y=2-e-x

Linear differential equation,

So, I.F. =e-∫dx=e-x

Now solution of differential equation is given by 

y×IF=∫2-e-x×IFdx

⇒ye-x=∫2-e-xe-xdx

⇒ye-x=∫2e-x-e-2xdx

⇒ye-x=-2e-x+e-2x2+C

⇒y=-2+e-x2+Cex

Given limx→∞y is finite

So, limx→∞-2+e-x2+C·ex→finite

This is possible only when C=0

Hence y=yx=-2+e-x2

Now finding the slope dydx=-12e-x

dydxx=0=-12=m,y0=-2+12=-32

Now equation of tangent will be,

y+32=-12x-0

⇒x+2y=-3

x-intercept a=-3, y-intercept b=-32

So, a-4b=-3+6=3

Given that: kf(k)+2=0 for k=2,3,4,5 which means (k-2),(k-3),(k-4),(k-5) are the factors of this expression.

a=160

kf(k)+2=160(k-2)(k-3)(k-4)(k-5)

Now, put k=10

10 f(10)=26

So, 52-10 f(10)=26

questions.q: Let <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">S</mi><mo>=</mo><mfenced close="}" open="{"><mrow><mn>1</mn><mo>,</mo><mn>2</mn><mo>,</mo><mn>3</mn><mo>,</mo><mn>4</mn><mo>,</mo><mn>5</mn><mo>,</mo><mn>6</mn><mo>,</mo><mn>7</mn></mrow></mfenced><mo>.</mo></math> Then the number of possible functions <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>f</mi><mo>:</mo><mi>S</mi><mo>→</mo><mi>S</mi></math> such that <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>f</mi><mfenced><mrow><mi>m</mi><mo>·</mo><mi>n</mi></mrow></mfenced><mo>=</mo><mi>f</mi><mfenced><mi>m</mi></mfenced><mo>·</mo><mi>f</mi><mfenced><mi>n</mi></mfenced></math> for every <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>m</mi><mo>,</mo><mi>n</mi><mo>∈</mo><mi>S</mi></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>m</mi><mo>·</mo><mi>n</mi><mo>∈</mo><mi>S</mi></math>, is equal to _____.
questions.solution:
Given,

fmn=fm·fn

Put m=1, then f1·n=f1·fn⇒f1=1 fn≠0

Put m=n=2

f4=f2·f2

If f2=1, then f4=1 and if f2=2, then f4=4

Put m=2,n=3

f6=f2·f3

If f2=1, then f3 can be any value between 1 to 7

If f2=2, then f3 can be 1 or 2 or 3

Similarly f5,f7 can take any value from 1,2,...,7

So, total number of function =1×1×7×1×7×1×7+1×1×3×1×7×1×7

=490

Then the number of functions <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>f</mi><mo>:</mo><mi>A</mi><mo>→</mo><mi>B</mi></math> satisfying <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>f</mi><mfenced><mn>1</mn></mfenced><mo>+</mo><mi>f</mi><mfenced><mn>2</mn></mfenced><mo>=</mo><mi>f</mi><mfenced><mn>4</mn></mfenced><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></math> is equal to........
questions.solution:
Given,

 f(1)+f2+1=f4

⇒f1+f2≤5 as f4≤6 

Now taking cases we get,

Case 1, f1=1⇒f2=1,2,3,4→4 mappings

Case 2, f1=2⇒f2=1,2,3→3 mappings

Case 3, f1=3⇒f2=1,2→2 mappings

Case 4, f1=4⇒f2=1→1 mappings

And f5 & f6→6 mapping each

So, total number of function will be =4+3+2+1×6×6=360

Then the number of elements in the set {<math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>f</mi><mo>:</mo><mi>S</mi><mo>×</mo><mi>S</mi><mo>→</mo><mi>S</mi><mo>:</mo><mi>f</mi></math> is onto and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>f</mi><mfenced><mrow><mi>a</mi><mo>,</mo><mi>b</mi></mrow></mfenced><mo>=</mo><mi>f</mi><mfenced><mrow><mi>b</mi><mo>,</mo><mi>a</mi></mrow></mfenced></math> <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mo>≥</mo><mi>a</mi><mo>∀</mo><mfenced><mrow><mi>a</mi><mo>,</mo><mi>b</mi></mrow></mfenced><mo>∈</mo><mi>S</mi><mo>×</mo><mi>S</mi></math>} is
questions.solution:
Finding the cases for fa,b=fb,a≥a we have,

1,1,1,4,4,1,2,4,4,2,3,4,4,3,4,4- all have one choice for image.

2,1,1,2,2,2- all have three choices for image.

3,2,2,3,3,1,1,3,3,3-all have two choices for image.

So the total functions =3×3×2×2×2=72

Case 1: None of the pre-images have 3 as image. 

Total functions =2×2×1×1×1=4

Case 2: None of the pre-images have 2 as image

Total functions =2×2×2×2×2=32

Case 3: None of the pre-images have either 3 or 2 as image

Total functions =1×1×1×1×1=1

∴ Total onto functions =72-4-32+1=37

questions.q: If <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><munder><mi>lim</mi><mrow><mi>x</mi><mo>→</mo><mn>0</mn></mrow></munder><mfenced close="]" open="["><mfrac><mrow><mi>α</mi><mi>x</mi><msup><mi>e</mi><mi>x</mi></msup><mo>-</mo><mi>β</mi><msub><mi>log</mi><mi>e</mi></msub><mo>(</mo><mn>1</mn><mo>+</mo><mi>x</mi><mo>)</mo><mo>+</mo><mi>γ</mi><msup><mi>x</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><msup><mi mathvariant="normal">e</mi><mrow><mo>-</mo><mi>x</mi></mrow></msup></mrow><mrow><mi>x</mi><msup><mi>sin</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mi>x</mi></mrow></mfrac></mfenced><mo>=</mo><mn>10</mn><mo>,</mo><mo> </mo><mi>α</mi><mo>,</mo><mo> </mo><mi>β</mi><mo>,</mo><mo> </mo><mi>γ</mi><mo>∈</mo><mi>R</mi></math>, then the value of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>α</mi><mo>+</mo><mi>β</mi><mo>+</mo><mi>γ</mi></math> is __________.
questions.solution:
We have,

limx→0αxex-βloge(1+x)+γx2e-xxsin2x=10

⇒limx→0αx1+x+x22!+..-βx-x22+x33-...+γx21-x+x22!-..x3sin2xx2=10

⇒limx→0αx1+x+x22-βx-x22+x33+γx21-xx3=10

Neglecting higher powers.

limx→0x(α-β)+x2α+β2+γ+x3α2-β3-γx3=10

For limit to exist

α-β=0

α+β2+γ=0

α2-β3-γ=10 …i

β=α,γ=-3α2

Putting in i, we get

α2-α3+3α2=10

⇒α6+3α2=10

⇒α+9α6=10

⇒α=6

Hence,

α=6,β=6,γ=-9

⇒α+β+γ=3

questions.q: Set <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>A</mi></math> has <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>m</mi></math>elements and set <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>B</mi></math> has <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>n</mi></math>elements. If the total number of subsets of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>A</mi></math> is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>112</mn></math> more than the total number of subsets of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>B</mi></math>, then the value of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>m</mi><mo>·</mo><mi>n</mi></math> is___.
questions.solution:
2m=2n+112
⇒2m-2n=112
⇒2n·2m-n-1=24·23-1
⇒n=4, m=7

questions.q: Let <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>A</mi><mo>=</mo><mfenced open="{" close="}"><mrow><mn>1</mn><mo>,</mo><mn>2</mn><mo>,</mo><mn>3</mn><mo>,</mo><mn>4</mn><mo>,</mo><mn>5</mn><mo>,</mo><mn>6</mn><mo>,</mo><mn>7</mn></mrow></mfenced></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>B</mi><mo>=</mo><mfenced open="{" close="}"><mrow><mn>3</mn><mo>,</mo><mn>6</mn><mo>,</mo><mn>7</mn><mo>,</mo><mn>9</mn></mrow></mfenced></math>. Then the number of elements in the set <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfenced open="{" close="}"><mrow><mi>C</mi><mo>⊆</mo><mi>A</mi><mo>:</mo><mi>C</mi><mo>∩</mo><mi>B</mi><mo>≠</mo><mi>ϕ</mi></mrow></mfenced></math> is ______
questions.solution:
Given,

A=1,2,3,4,5,6,7 and B=3,6,7,9

We know that if any set has n elements then total number of subset is given by 2n

So, total subset of A=27=128

C∩B=ϕ when set C contains the element 1,2,4,5

∴  S=C⊆A;C∩B≠ϕ

= Total-C∩B=ϕ

=128-24=128-16=112

questions.q: Let <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>A</mi><mo>=</mo><mfenced open="{" close="}"><mrow><mn>1</mn><mo>,</mo><mn>2</mn><mo>,</mo><mn>3</mn><mo>,</mo><mn>4</mn><mo>,</mo><mn>5</mn><mo>,</mo><mn>6</mn><mo>,</mo><mn>7</mn></mrow></mfenced></math>. Define <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>B</mi><mo>=</mo></math>{<math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>T</mi><mo>⊆</mo><mi>A</mi></math>: either <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>1</mn><mo>∉</mo><mi>T</mi></math> or <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>2</mn><mo>∈</mo><mi>T</mi></math>} and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>C</mi><mo>=</mo></math>{<math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>T</mi><mo>⊆</mo><mi>A</mi><mo>:</mo><mi>T</mi></math> the sum of all the elements of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>T</mi></math> is a prime number.} Then the number of elements in the set <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>B</mi><mo>∪</mo><mi>C</mi></math> is _______.
questions.solution:
We know that,

By demorgan's law B∪C'=B'∩C'

B' is a set containing sub sets of A containing element 1 and not containing 2.

And C' is a set containing subsets of A whose sum of elements is not prime.

So, we need to calculate number of subsets of 3,4,5,6,7 whose sum of elements plus 1 is composite.

Number of such 5 elements subset =1

Number of such 4 elements subset =3 (except selecting 3 or 7)

Number of such 3 elements subset =6 (except selecting 3,4,5,3,6,7,4,5,7 or 5,6,7)

Number of such 2 elements subset =7 (except selecting 3,7,4,6,5,7)

Number of such 1 elements subset =3 (except selecting 4 or 6)

Number of such 0 elements subset =1

So, number of elements in nB'∩C'=1+3+6+7+3+1=21

And number of elements in nB∩C=27-21=107

questions.q: Let <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>A</mi><mo>=</mo><mfenced open="{" close="}"><mrow><mn>1</mn><mo>,</mo><msub><mi>a</mi><mn>1</mn></msub><mo>,</mo><msub><mi>a</mi><mn>2</mn></msub><mo>…</mo><mo>…</mo><msub><mi>a</mi><mn>18</mn></msub><mo>,</mo><mn>77</mn></mrow></mfenced></math> be a set of integers with <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>1</mn><mo><</mo><msub><mi>a</mi><mn>1</mn></msub><mo><</mo><msub><mi>a</mi><mn>2</mn></msub><mo><</mo><mo>…</mo><mo>.</mo><mo>.</mo><mo><</mo><msub><mi>a</mi><mn>18</mn></msub><mo><</mo><mn>77</mn></math>. Let the set <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>A</mi><mo>+</mo><mi>A</mi><mo>=</mo><mfenced open="{" close="}"><mrow><mi>x</mi><mo>+</mo><mi>y</mi><mo>:</mo><mi>x</mi><mo>,</mo><mi>y</mi><mo>∈</mo><mi>A</mi></mrow></mfenced></math> contain exactly <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>39</mn></math> elements. Then, the value of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>a</mi><mn>1</mn></msub><mo>+</mo><msub><mi>a</mi><mn>2</mn></msub><mo>+</mo><mo>…</mo><mo>.</mo><mo>.</mo><mo>+</mo><msub><mi>a</mi><mn>18</mn></msub></math> is equal to ______.
questions.solution:
If we write the elements of \(A+A\), we can certainly find 39 distinct elements as \(1+1,1+a_1, 1+a_2, \ldots .1\) \(+a_{18}, 1+77, a_1+77, a_2+77, \ldots \ldots a_{18}+77,77+77\). It means all other sums are already present in these 39 values, which is only possible in case when all numbers are in A.P.
Let the common difference be ' \(d\) '.
\(77=1+19 d \Rightarrow d=4\)
So, \(\sum_{i=1}^{18} a_1=\frac{18}{2}\left[2 a_1+17 d\right]=9[10+68]=702\)

questions.q: Let <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>A</mi><mo>=</mo><mfenced close="}" open="{"><mrow><mn>1</mn><mo>,</mo><mo> </mo><mn>2</mn><mo>,</mo><mo> </mo><mn>3</mn><mo>,</mo><mo> </mo><mn>4</mn></mrow></mfenced></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>R</mi></math> be a relation on the set <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>A</mi><mo>×</mo><mi>A</mi></math> defined by <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>R</mi><mo>=</mo><mfenced close="}" open="{"><mrow><mfenced><mrow><mfenced><mrow><mi>a</mi><mo>,</mo><mo> </mo><mi>b</mi></mrow></mfenced><mo>,</mo><mo> </mo><mfenced><mrow><mi>c</mi><mo>,</mo><mo> </mo><mi>d</mi></mrow></mfenced></mrow></mfenced><mo>:</mo><mn>2</mn><mi>a</mi><mo>+</mo><mn>3</mn><mi>b</mi><mo>=</mo><mn>4</mn><mi>c</mi><mo>+</mo><mn>5</mn><mi>d</mi></mrow></mfenced></math>. Then the number of elements in <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>R</mi></math> is _________.
questions.solution:
Given,

Set A=1,2,3,4

And relation on A×A as 2a+3b=4c+5d

Now, Maximum value of 2a+3b=20 at 4, 4

And Minimum value of 4c+5d=9 at 1, 1

So, 4c+5d can be equal to 9, 13, 14, 17, 18, 19

Now 2a+3b can be 9 if  a, b=3, 1⇒c, d=1, 1

Similarly, 2a+3b can be 13, if a, b=2, 3⇒c, d=2, 1

And 2a+3b can be 14, if a, b=4, 2  or 1, 4

⇒c, d=1, 2

Similarly, 2a+3b can be 17, if a, b=4, 3⇒ c, d=3, 1

And 2a+3b can be 18 if a, b=3, 4⇒

c, d=2, 2

Hence, there are total 6 elements which satisfy the given relation.

questions.q: Let <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>X</mi><mo>=</mo><mfenced close="}" open="{" separators="|"><mrow><mi>n</mi><mo>∈</mo><mi>N</mi><mo>:</mo><mn>1</mn><mo>≤</mo><mi>n</mi><mo>≤</mo><mn>50</mn></mrow></mfenced></math>. If <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>A</mi><mo>=</mo><mfenced open="{" close="}" separators="|"><mrow><mi>n</mi><mo>∈</mo><mi>X</mi><mo>:</mo><mo> </mo><mi>n </mi><mi mathvariant="normal">i</mi><mi>s </mi><mi>a </mi><mi mathvariant="normal">m</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">u</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">l</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">t</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">i</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">p</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">l</mi><mi>e </mi><mi mathvariant="normal">o</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">f</mi><mo> </mo><mn>2</mn></mrow></mfenced></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">B</mi><mo>=</mo><mfenced open="{" close="}" separators="|"><mrow><mi>n</mi><mo>∈</mo><mi>X</mi><mo>:</mo><mo> </mo><mi>n </mi><mi mathvariant="normal">i</mi><mi>s </mi><mi>a </mi><mi mathvariant="normal">m</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">u</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">l</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">t</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">i</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">p</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">l</mi><mi>e </mi><mi mathvariant="normal">o</mi><mi>f </mi><mn>7</mn></mrow></mfenced></math>, then the number of elements in the smallest subset of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>X</mi></math>, containing both <math><mi mathvariant="normal">A</mi></math> and <math><mi mathvariant="normal">B</mi></math>, is.
questions.solution:
Here, A=2,4,6,....50 and B=7,14,...49

Required smallest subset is A∪B.

So, nA∪B=nA+nB-nA∩B
=25+7-3
=29.
